_cuda_behavior_notes.decode_ratio_expectation: turbo3/q8_0 should stay >= 0.90x at ALL context depths on CUDA (no Metal constant-cache degradation)
_cuda_behavior_notes.prefill_ratio_expectation: turbo3/q8_0 should stay flat 0.98-1.00x across all depths
_cuda_behavior_notes.stress_test_note: Section 8 stress results should show no inflection point — CUDA gather is not limited by Metal's constant cache

qwen35_27b_q5km.quantization_stacking_risk: LOW — Q5_K_M weights are high quality; symmetric turbo should work fine
notes: Q5_K_M (~20GB) fits across both GPUs with ~4.5GB headroom for KV cache
turbo4 KV enables 128K context within the ~24.5GB VRAM budget
Q8_0 (~29GB) requires both GPUs at capacity; limit ctx to 32K

qwen35_27b_q4km.quantization_stacking_risk: MEDIUM — Q4_K_M weights + symmetric turbo KV can trigger PPL catastrophe on some models (validated on Qwen2.5-7B: PPL 6.58 → 3556 with turbo3/turbo3). Larger models appear more tolerant.
qwen35_27b_q4km.validation_command: llama-perplexity -m model.gguf -ngl 99 --tensor-split 0.63,0.37 -fa on -ctk turbo4 -ctv turbo4 --chunks 8 -c 512 -f wiki.test.raw
qwen35_27b_q4km.rescue_strategy: If PPL > 10% above q8_0 baseline, switch to: -ctk q8_0 -ctv turbo4 (K stays high-precision, V gets 2x compression)

_asymmetric_kv_guidance.discovery: K precision is the dominant quality factor. K errors cascade through softmax exponentially. V errors are linearly proportional in the attention output.

q8_0_k_plus_turbo4_v.description: Safe fallback for any model weight quantization level

turbo4_k_plus_turbo4_v.description: Symmetric compression — fine for Q5_K_M and above

turbo3_k_plus_turbo3_v.description: Maximum compression — validate PPL before production use
turbo3_k_plus_turbo3_v.risk: Higher stacking sensitivity for Q4_K_M models

_build_instructions.cuda_arch_note: On mixed Blackwell(sm_100) + Ampere(sm_86), cmake will compile for both automatically
_build_instructions.cuda_mmq_hint: GGML_CUDA_FORCE_MMQ=1 can improve throughput for batch=1 inference on this mixed setup
